bones.name: Anterior view
description: The anterior view is generally ovoid in shape.
The forehead is superior to the orbit and is formed by the frontal bone.
The bones that make up the borders of the orbit are the frontal bone superiorly, medially, and laterally, the zygomatic bone laterally and inferiorly, and the maxilla inferior and medially.
The anterior nasal opening is formed by the nasal and maxilla bones.
The maxilla contributes to the upper jaw, while the lower jaw is formed by the mandible.

bones.name: Frontal bone
description: A slight depression that lies between the two
It can be more pronounced in males.
An opening at the superior rim of the orbit that transmits the supraorbital nerve and vessels.
Lies at the junction where the frontal bone meets the nasal bones.
The lateral part of the bone that extends inferiorly to join with the zygomatic bone.
Forms part of the lateral border of the orbit.

bones.name: Sphenoid bone
description: Anteriorly, the sphenoid bone can be seen in the deep part of the orbit.
A gap formed by the lesser and greater wings of the sphenoid bone.
Transmits the oculomotor nerve (CN III), trochlear nerve (CN IV), branches of the ophthalmic nerve (CN V
), abducent nerve (CN VI), and the superior ophthalmic vein.
Transmits the optic nerve and the ophthalmic artery.

bones.name: Nasal bone
description: Two small, oblong shaped bones that form the “bridge” of the nose.

bones.name: Zygomatic bone
description: An irregular shaped bone that, together with the maxilla, forms the “cheek” bones.
The portion of bone that extends superiorly to join with the frontal bone.
Contributes to the lateral border of the orbit.
A small opening just inferior and lateral to the orbit.
Transmits the zygomaticofacial nerve and vessels.
The portion of bone that extends laterally and posteriorly to join the temporal bone.
Forms part of the zygomatic arch.

bones.name: Maxilla
description: Two bones that make up the central part of the face and upper jaw.
Part of the bone that extends superiorly to join the frontal and nasal bones.
A small opening just inferior to the inferior orbit rim.
Transmits the infraorbital nerve and vessels.
Part of the bone that extends laterally to meet with the zygomatic bone.
Part of bone that extends inferiorly and forms upper jaw.

bones.name: Labels
description: Bone that forms the lower jaw and contains the lower teeth.
Horizontal part the bone that fuses with the bone from the opposite side during development.
Part of bone that projects superiorly from the posterior part of the body.
An opening in the body of the mandible that lies in the same sagittal plane as the supraorbital and infraorbital foramina.
Transmits the mental nerve and vessels.
Part of bone that slightly extends anteriorly.
The mental tubercles on each side produce the characteristic prominence of the chin.

bones.name: No labels
description: The lateral view can be divided into 3 sections:
The temporal section is separated by the zygomatic arch into into a
superiorly and an infratemporal fossa inferiorly.
The origin of the temporalis muscle attaches to the temporal fossa
is located in the temporal fossa and marks the junction where the frontal, parietal, temporal, and sphenoid bones meet.
The pterion is an important landmark because it overlies the anterior branch of the middle meningeal artery.
Damage to this area of the skull can cause a rupture of this artery

bones.name: Parietal bone
description: Makes up the large, curved portion superior, lateral and posterior skull.
Joins with the posterior part of the frontal bone at the
Joins with the superior part of the occipital bone at the

bones.name: Sphenoid bone
description: The greater wing of the sphenoid bone can be seen from the lateral view.
The greater wing forms part of the medial wall of the temporal fossa and the roof of the infratemporal fossa.

bones.name: Ethmoid bone
description: The ethmoid bone forms part of the medial wall of the orbit.

bones.name: Lacrimal bone
description: The lacrimal bone forms part of the medial wall of the orbit.
A shallow depression formed by the frontal process of the maxilla and the lacrimal bone.
The lacrimal sac is the dilated portion of the nasolacrimal duct and is part of an apparatus to drain tears from the surface of the eye to the inferior nasal meatus.

bones.name: Labels
description: The flattened part of the temporal bone that makes up much of the floor (medial wall) of the temporal fossa.
Part of the bone that extends anteriorly to join with the temporal process of the zygomatic bone.
Together, these form the zygomatic arch.
An inferior projection where the insertion of the sternocleidomastoid muscle attaches.
The external opening for the ear.
A shallow depression that receives the head of the condylar process of the mandible to form the temporomandibular joint.

bones.name: Labels
description: Is quadrangular in shape and projects superiorly from the posterior part of the body of the mandible.
The insertion of the masseter muscle attaches to the lateral surface of the ramus.
The posterior projection of the ramus.
Distal portion has a head that articulates with the condylar fossa of the temporal bone to form the temporomandibular joint.
The anterior projection of the ramus.
The temporalis muscle inserts on the coronoid process.
Space created by the separation of the two processes of the ramus.
The part of the bone where the ramus and inferior borders meet.

bones.name: Cranial base
description: The base of the skull extends from the upper teeth anteriorly to the superior nuchal line of the occipital bone posteriorly.

bones.name: Maxilla
description: The inferior portion of each maxilla that makes up the anterior part of the hard palate.
The joint between the palatine processes on each side.
The joint between the palatine processes of the maxilla and the horizontal plates of the palatine bones.
An opening in the midline of the anterior part of the hard palate.
Transmits the nasopalatine nerve and the sphenopalatine vessels.

bones.name: Labels
description: Projects inferiorly from the junction of the body and the greater wing of the sphenoid bone.
Composed of medial and lateral pterygoid plates separated by the pterygoid fossae.
The origins of the medial and lateral pterygoid muscles are from either side of the lateral plate respectively.
The tensor veli palatini muscle arises partially from the scaphoid fossa at the base of the medial plate and hooks around the hamulus.
An oval opening at the base of the lateral plate.
) of the trigeminal nerve (CN V) as well as the lesser petrosal nerve and the accessory meningeal artery.
A smaller opening just posterior and lateral to the foramen ovale.
Transmits the middle meningeal artery and the meningeal branch of the mandibular nerve

bones.name: Palatine bone
description: Forms the posterior part of the hard palate.
An opening just lateral to the third molars of the upper jaw.
Transmits the greater palatine nerve and vessels.
A smaller opening that lies posterior to the greater palatine foramen.
Usually found in the pyramid process of palatine bone.
Transmits the lesser palatine nerve and vessels.

bones.name: Labels
description: A fissure between the mandibular fossa and the tympanic part of the temporal bone.
Transmits the chorda tympani branch of the facial nerve (CN VII).
A small opening in the petrous part medial to the styloid process.
Transmits the internal carotid artery and the carotid autonomic plexus.
A sharp protrusion that extends inferiorly and anteriorly from the petrous part.
Three muscles take origin here, they are: styloglossus, stylohyoid, and stylopharyngeus.
An opening between the styloid and mastoid processes.
A rounded protrusion that extends inferiorly from the lateral part of the temporal bone just behind the external acoustic meatus.
Serves as an attachment for splenius and longissimus capitus, posterior belly of the digastric, and the sternocleidomastoid muscles.

bones.name: Vomer
description: The posterior part of the vomer lies between the medial plates of the sphenoid bones on either side.
It contributes to the inferior and posterior part of the nasal septum.
The posterior openings of the nasal passages.

bones.name: Labels
description: The superior surface of the orbit.

bones.name: Labels
description: A perforated plate of bone that lies between the two orbital plates of the frontal bone.
Contains foramina that transmit the olfactory nerves (CN I).
A crest of bone that projects superiorly from the midline.
The attachment point for the falx cerebri.
– a crescent shaped sheet of meningeal dura mater that contains the superior sagittal sinus.
An opening between the ethmoid and frontal bones that transmits an emissary vein which bridges the nasal cavity and the superior sagittal sinus.

bones.name: Labels
description: A depression that contains the pituitary gland and part of the cavernous sinus.
An opening that projects anteriorly to the orbit.
Transmits the optic nerve (CN II) and the ophthalmic artery.
An opening in the medial part of the greater wing.
An opening posterior to the foramen rotundum.
), the accessory meningeal artery, and the lesser petrosal nerve.
A small opening just posterior to the foramen ovale.
Transmits the middle meningeal artery and the meningeal branch of the mandibular nerve

bones.name: Labels
description: An elevated portion of bone that resembles a mountain.
separates the middle cranial fossa from the posterior cranial fossa.
The flattened part of bone that extends superiorly towards the lateral part of the skull.
An opening in the medial side of the petrous part just superior and anterior to the jugular foramen.
Transmits the facial nerve (CN VII), the vestibulocochlear nerve (CN VIII), and the labyrinthine artery.
A rounded elevation on the superior aspect of the petrous part.
Indicates the position of the underlying anterior semicircular canal of inner ear.

bones.name: Labels
description: A large opening in the created by the joining of the occipital bone and the petrous part of the temporal bone.
Transmits the internal jugular vein, the glossopharyngeal nerve (CN IX), the vagus nerve (CN X), the accessory nerve (CN XI), and the posterior meningeal artery.
A large opening in the anterior and midline part of the occipital bone.
Transmits the medulla of the brainstem, the vertebral vessels, and the spinal roots of the accessory nerve (CN XI).
An opening just medial and inferior to the jugular foramen and superior to the margins of the foramen magnum.
